channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kenya Faces Temporary Shortage of Rotavac Vaccine
item.description: The Ministry of Health in Kenya has issued a warning that the country is facing a temporary shortage of the Rotavac rotavirus vaccine following disruptions in global supply. Currently, only about 4,00...

item.title: Experts Advise Women to Use Loans Responsibly for Economic Growth
item.description: On 6th March 2026, during a breakfast event organized by CRDB Bank in collaboration with Mwananchi Communications Limited to mark International Women’s Day, CRDB Bank Business Development Manager, Mar...

item.title: Cross River State Allocates N2 Billion for Governor’s Lodge Renovation Following Previous Expenditure
item.description: Di Cross River State government don plan to spend N2 billion on the renovation of di governor’s lodge, just nine months after dem don already spend N994 million on top di same lodge between January an...

item.title: Helen Zille outlines plan to create 200 000 jobs and improve services in Johannesburg
item.description: Democratic Alliance mayoral candidate for Johannesburg, Helen Zille, has officially launched her 'Get Joburg Working' campaign, placing strong emphasis on job creation and improving municipal services...

item.title: Tinubu Approves New Ambassadors for Various Countries Including Reno Omokri to Mexico
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu don approve new ambassador postings for Nigeria, including both career diplomats and non-career appointees. This approval follow Senate confirmation wey happen for December...

item.title: Ellyse Perry sets new record as Australia’s top run-scorer in women’s Test cricket
item.description: Ellyse Perry has cemented her place in Australian cricket history by becoming the nation’s all-time leading run-scorer in women’s Test matches. She achieved this milestone during the day-night Test ag...

item.title: Lessons from Harold Wilson for Keir Starmer on Managing Tense US-UK Relations
item.description: The Anglo-American 'special relationship' has recently suffered strain following US strikes on Iran, with President Donald Trump publicly criticising UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer for not providing m...

item.title: Reflecting on 25 Years of Noughts & Crosses: Malorie Blackman on Racism, Literature and Legacy
item.description: Malorie Blackman, one of Britain's most influential contemporary writers, reflects on the 25th anniversary of her seminal young adult novel, Noughts & Crosses. The book, inspired by racism in 1990s Br...

item.title: Over 52,000 Passengers Repatriated from Gulf to India by 280 Flights Amid Regional Disruptions
item.description: As of Friday 11:59 pm IST, more than 52,000 passengers have been flown from the Gulf countries to India on a combination of scheduled and charter flights, according to the Directorate General of Civil...

item.title: Indian Shia Community Mourns Khamenei Amid Global Tensions Following His Assassination
item.description: The assassination of Iran’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, on 28 February 2026, in an air strike by Israel and the US, has reverberated far beyond Tehran, sparking protests and mourning acros...

item.title: Clarifying Land Compensation Processes for Project Affected Persons in Tanzania
item.description: This article talks about how in Tanzania, when the government or a company wants to expand projects like mines, land is sometimes acquired compulsorily. Many people confuse the process, thinking that ...

item.title: PAP Administrator Commends South-South Governors for Smooth APC Ward and State Congresses
item.description: Dr Dennis Brutu Otuaro, wey be the Administrator of the Presidential Amnesty Programme (PAP), don commend all the South-South governors for how dem take manage the recent APC ward and state congresses...

item.title: Carolina Hurricanes defeat Edmonton Oilers with coordinated team play
item.description: The Carolina Hurricanes extended their strong season performance with a 6-3 victory over the Edmonton Oilers. The Hurricanes, who lead the Eastern Conference, showcased exceptional teamwork, with Jack...

item.title: New Zealand prepares to challenge India in T20 World Cup final
item.description: New Zealand cricket team is all set to face hosts and defending champions India in the T20 World Cup final on Sunday at Ahmedabad's Narendra Modi Stadium, which is expected to be packed with over 100,...

item.title: From politics to farming: ex-Bomet MCA builds a solid dairy enterprise after losing his seat
item.description: This article tells the story of a former Bomet ward representative who turned an electoral defeat into a fresh start in dairy farming. After losing his political seat, he chose not to remain stuck in ...

item.title: ALMA Maps Cold Gas Filaments in the Milky Way’s Central Region
item.description: Astronomers using the Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA) have created the most detailed image yet of the Milky Way's central region, known as the Central Molecular Zone (CMZ). Spannin...

item.title: Australian Involvement in US-Iran Tensions Raises Concerns Over International Law Compliance
item.description: The Albanese government is facing criticism over Australia's potential complicity in US-led military actions against Iran, following the sinking of an Iranian ship off Sri Lanka by a US submarine. Rep...

item.title: Challenges in Tanzania’s Microfinance Sector Affect Lending Capacity
item.description: Tanzania's microfinance sector, wey long time serve as main help for small businesses, farmers, and low-income borrowers, inah face serious wahala with cash availability, government rules, and loan re...

item.title: Ian Huntley, convicted Soham murderer, dies after attack in prison
item.description: Ian Huntley, the convicted murderer of 10-year-olds Holly Wells and Jessica Chapman, has died in hospital following a violent attack in prison. The 52-year-old was found in a pool of blood on 26 Febru...

item.title: Joeboy talks about challenges new artists face in breaking into the music industry
item.description: Afrobeats singer Joeboy don raise alarm on how e hard for new artists wey wan make name for music industry now. For recent interview with Jay On Air, e talk say the wahala don increase mainly because ...
